data.title: Anthropic CEO to Meet with White House Chief of Staff on Friday
data.description: Anthropic's CEO is scheduled to meet with the White House Chief of Staff this Friday. According to Odaily, the meeting will take place at the White House. Further details about the agenda or topics of discussion have not been disclosed.

data.title: Binance Wallet plugin now supports mnemonic phrase derived accounts.
data.description: According to official sources, the Binance Wallet plugin now supports generating accounts from mnemonic phrases. With just one set of mnemonic phrases backed up, up to 99 independent accounts can be generated. This protects mnemonic phrase security, enables efficient account management, and supports multiple chains, allowing a single account to be used across chains. The plugin supports versions 1.12.0 and above.

data.title: Survey Reveals Stablecoin Use Cases and Trust in Major Financial Institutions
data.description:
A recent survey conducted by Nomura and Laser Digital among 518 investment professionals in Japan highlights the growing recognition of stablecoin use cases. According to NS3.AI, 63% of respondents identified stablecoin applications, with major financial institutions issuing JPY, USD, and EUR products garnering the highest trust.

SBI VC Trade's planned distribution of RLUSD remains pending approval, as indicated in late 2025 materials. Meanwhile, MUFG Bank, Mizuho Bank, SMBC, Mitsubishi UFJ Trust, and Progmat are progressing with a joint stablecoin proof of concept, showcasing the collaborative efforts in advancing stablecoin technology.

data.title: SIREN manipulators withdrew 1.369 million tokens from the Binance Alpha wallet, bringing the token concentration to over 93%.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, manipulators withdrew 1.369 million $SIREN tokens, worth $2.45 million, from the Binance Alpha wallet in the past 6 hours, currently holding over 93% of the tokens. The wallet addresses are: 0xB57a00f0C46585db22926C365B849ec7699f6a45 and 0x1f979198620F07Bb20EC49fB00530fb0fA3430bA.

data.title: Gulf Bank in Singapore launches stablecoin minting and redemption service
data.description: Gulf Bank (SGB) of Singapore today announced the launch of its stablecoin issuance and redemption service. Corporate and high-net-worth clients can now directly exchange fiat currency for stablecoins through their SGB accounts, achieving 24/7 instant settlement without relying on traditional correspondent banking processes. To support the launch, SGB is pioneering a new approach in the banking industry by waiving gas and bank fees for users minting and redeeming tokens on Solana for a limited time. At the end of the promotion, clients will also receive rewards based on their trading volume. SGB CEO, Tan Siang, stated: “The challenges of cross-border capital transfers and placement have become a key factor limiting customer growth. By directly integrating stablecoin issuance/redemption into the banking system, we have achieved real-time conversion between fiat currency and digital assets. We are building a bank for a borderless world.” This service is integrated into SGB’s proprietary clearing network, SGB Net, supporting seamless fund flows in both on-chain and off-chain environments. Initially, USDC transactions exceeding $100,000 are supported, with USDT, USDe (Ethena), and Global Dollar (USDG) to follow.

data.title: Bitcoin's RHODL ratio has risen to its third-highest level in history, potentially indicating that a bottom has been formed.
data.description: Glassnode's on-chain Bitcoin metric, the RHODL ratio, is currently at 4.5, the third highest level on record, signaling a market bottom rather than a cycle top. The RHODL ratio compares the value of Bitcoin held by long-term holders (6 months to 3 years) to short-term holders (1 day to 3 months). A rising ratio typically reflects longer holding periods and reduced speculative activity, rather than an influx of new buyers—a dynamic that occurred after the significant corrections in 2015, 2019, and 2022. During Bitcoin's 50% drop over the past six months, a large amount of speculative capital was wiped out, and wealth concentrated in the hands of long-term holders. Historically, the RHODL ratio has only been higher than it is now twice: in 2015 (5) and 2022 (7), both corresponding to cycle bottoms, suggesting that Bitcoin may still have further downside potential. However, for the ratio to rise to higher levels, short-term holder activity typically needs to almost completely dry up, which is not yet evident under current conditions—Bitcoin has rebounded by about 25% from its February lows, perpetual contract funding rates remain negative, and the S&P 500 has hit an all-time high. Overall, this indicator suggests that the current market situation is closer to a cyclical correction than a cyclical top, and the renewed dominance of long-term holders may indicate that a temporary bottom is approaching. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Binance Wallet Extension Adds Seed Phrase Derived Accounts
data.description:
Binance Wallet announced on X that its extension now supports seed phrase derived accounts, allowing users to manage up to 99 independent accounts with a single seed phrase backup. This update aims to enhance security and efficiency in account management.

The new feature enables each derived account to function seamlessly across multiple blockchain networks, providing users with greater flexibility in managing their digital assets. Users can easily search for accounts by name or address, streamlining the process of locating specific accounts quickly.

To access these new capabilities, users are advised to update their wallet extension to version 1.12.0 or above. This upgrade is essential to ensure compatibility with the latest features and improvements. The announcement highlights the importance of keeping the seed phrase secure, as it is crucial for managing multiple accounts effectively.

data.title: Crypto Job Postings Decline 80% Amid AI Adoption and Bear Market
data.description: Crypto job postings have decreased by 80% year over year, according to NS3.AI. This decline is attributed to the increasing adoption of AI and the ongoing bear market, which have significantly influenced hiring trends in the sector. While stablecoins continue to be a strong area for recruitment, many exchanges and crypto firms are reducing their workforce. These companies are now focusing on roles related to compliance, security, and AI, reflecting a shift in industry priorities.

data.title: Singapore Gulf Bank Launches Stablecoin Services for High Net Worth Clients
data.description:
Singapore Gulf Bank (SGB) has introduced stablecoin minting and redemption services for corporate and high-net-worth clients. According to Foresight News, these clients can now directly exchange fiat currency and stablecoins through their SGB accounts, enabling instant settlement around the clock. Initially, the service supports USDC transactions exceeding $100,000 and will later include USDT, USDe, and USDG.

To facilitate the launch, SGB is temporarily waiving gas fees and bank charges for token minting and redemption on the Solana blockchain. Additionally, clients can earn rewards based on transaction volume. SGB CEO Chen Shaoen stated that the initiative aims to achieve real-time conversion between fiat and digital assets, enhancing cash flow and fund management.

data.title: SIREN Token Experiences Significant Price Rebound
data.description: SIREN token has shown a notable recovery in its price. According to Odaily, after hitting a low of 0.258 USDT, the token rebounded to 0.7 USDT. The 24-hour decline has narrowed to 14.45%.

data.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Decline by 2%
data.description: Brent crude oil prices have experienced a significant drop, with a daily decrease of 2%. According to Odaily, the current price stands at $92.38 per barrel.

data.title: China Criticizes Japanese Vessel's Presence in Taiwan Strait
data.description: China has expressed disapproval over the presence of a Japanese Self-Defense Forces vessel in the Taiwan Strait, labeling it as a provocative act. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the tensions between the two nations over the strategic waterway. The Taiwan Strait is a significant geopolitical hotspot, with both China and Japan having vested interests in the region. This incident adds to the ongoing complexities in the East Asian maritime dynamics, where territorial and navigational rights are frequently contested. The situation underscores the delicate balance of power and the potential for diplomatic friction in the area.

data.title: SIREN rebounded to 0.7 USDT after falling to a low of 0.25 USDT.
data.description: Market data shows that SIREN rebounded to 0.7 USDT after falling to a low of 0.258 USDT, with the 24-hour decline narrowing to 14.45%.

data.title: Whale Acquires 4.5 Million LDO in OTC Deal
data.description: On-chain analyst Yu Jian reported that a significant transaction occurred involving a whale purchasing 4.5 million LDO in an over-the-counter (OTC) deal valued at $1.86 million. According to NS3.AI, the same wallet also received an additional 4.5 million LDO and 10,000 AAVE from Wintermute and FalconX within the past day.

data.title: Market Conditions Indicate Cycle Corrections, Not Late-Stage Tops
data.description:
The RHODL ratio, a key metric in the cryptocurrency market, indicates that current market conditions are more akin to cycle corrections rather than late-stage tops. According to CoinDesk, this assessment comes as long-term holders regain their dominance in the market. The RHODL ratio, which compares the wealth held by long-term holders to that of short-term holders, suggests a shift in market dynamics. This shift is characterized by the increasing influence of long-term investors, who are typically seen as more stable and less likely to react to short-term market fluctuations.

This trend is significant as it points to a market environment that is stabilizing rather than overheating. The dominance of long-term holders often signals a more mature market, where investors are less driven by speculative behavior. This can lead to more sustainable growth patterns, as opposed to the rapid spikes and declines often associated with speculative trading. The current market conditions, as indicated by the RHODL ratio, suggest that the market is undergoing a healthy correction phase, which could pave the way for future growth. This is a positive sign for the cryptocurrency market, as it indicates a move towards greater stability and maturity.

data.title: Middle East's Luxury Watch Market Faces Challenges Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The Middle East, once a thriving market for luxury timepieces, is experiencing significant challenges due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the region's demand for high-end watches has been impacted by the geopolitical tensions. The luxury watch industry, which had seen growth in the Middle East, is now facing uncertainty as the conflict disrupts economic stability and consumer confidence. Retailers and manufacturers are closely monitoring the situation, as the prolonged instability could further affect sales and market dynamics. The luxury sector, known for its resilience, is adapting to these challenges by exploring alternative markets and strategies to sustain growth.
